articles.author: Frank  Andrews
articles.title: Is Europe embracing air conditioning as deadly heat waves become more common? - CBS News
articles.description: Only 20% European homes have AC, compared to 90% in the U.S., but as the climate changes, that vast gulf may be set to shrink.
articles.content: London — Many Europeans have long seen air conditioning as an unnecessary, costly, carbon emissions-heavy indulgence. But as the continent's summers get hotter, claiming more lives as they do, that a… [+7726 chars]
